title: ubuntu 18.04 can't add ppa:projectatomic/ppa repository
body:
When I created `buildah` in Ubuntu 18.04, I found that I could not add the `ppa:projectatomic/ppa` repository. The details are as follows:
```
➜  ~ sudo -E apt-add-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa
Ign:1 http://dl.google.com/linux/chrome/deb stable InRelease
Get:2 http://dl.google.com/linux/chrome/deb stable Release [1,189 B]           
Hit:4 http://ppa.launchpad.net/git-core/ppa/ubuntu bionic InRelease                                       
Hit:5 https://mirrors.ustc.edu.cn/ubuntu bionic InRelease                                                 
Hit:6 https://mirrors.ustc.edu.cn/ubuntu bionic-updates InRelease              
Hit:7 http://ppa.launchpad.net/noobslab/icons/ubuntu bionic InRelease    
Hit:8 https://mirrors.ustc.edu.cn/ubuntu bionic-backports InRelease      
Hit:9 https://mirrors.ustc.edu.cn/ubuntu bionic-security InRelease       
Hit:10 https://mirrors.ustc.edu.cn/ubuntu bionic-proposed InRelease      
Ign:11 http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu bionic InRelease
Err:12 http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu bionic Release
  404  Not Found [IP: *********]
Reading package lists... Done
E: The repository 'http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu bionic Release' does not have a Release file.
N: Updating from such a repository can't be done securely, and is therefore disabled by default.
N: See apt-secure(8) manpage for repository creation and user configuration details.
```

Is this repository not yet providing support for 18.04? Is there any good solution?

（I don't know which side to report on this issue. I didn't find any better place, so I can only ask this question in this community. Hope you don't mind.）

comments.body: This is a problem with Launchpad, report it to them.
@lsm5 any further advice/details?
@q384566678 well, the PPA is only enabled for 16.04 (the previous LTS) so far. @fkluknav is working on 18.04 builds right now.
Thanks for the info @lsm5, I thought it was a WIP, but didn't want to overpromise.  WIll assign this to @fkluknav

Thank you for your information. Wait for the final result.
@lsm5 If it supports 18.04, where can I get the latest information?
@lsm5 Could you help out @q384566678
+1 vote for 18.04 support
+1 another vote for 18.04 support
@fernando-redhat you still seeing issues with 18.04 addition? can you post the logs please?

still actual

care to elaborate please?
@lsm5 trying to install on Ubuntu 18 errors the same as topicstarter written

> @lsm5 trying to install on Ubuntu 18 errors the same as topicstarter written

@terion-name is it 18.04 (bionic) or 18.10 (cosmic) ? If it's 18.10 (cosmic), then it's been [officially EOL-ed](https://fridge.ubuntu.com/2019/07/19/ubuntu-18-10-cosmic-cuttlefish-end-of-life-reached-on-july-18-2019/) and there's no point creating packages for it. Please consider upgrading to 19.04 (disco) in that case.
@lsm5 bionic

> @lsm5 bionic

@terion-name that's strange. It's working for me and I haven't heard anyone else complain about that recently. Mind posting the output here when you try to add the ppa please? The original post is more than a year old btw, so I'm not sure whatsup in your case, so the error output would certainly help. Thanks.

@lsm5

Also if to look here: http://ppa.launchpad.net/thumbor/ppa/ubuntu/dists/
There are only:

| Name | Last modified | Size | Description |

| devel/ | 2014-01-07 21:04 | - |  |
| precise/ | 2013-01-15 20:09 | - | |
| quantal/ | 2013-01-16 17:14 | - |  |
| raring/ | 2014-01-07 21:04 | - |  |
| saucy/ | 2014-01-07 21:04 | - |  |

Don't know if there should be `bionic`, but worth mentioning

On Thu, Sep 26, 2019 at 01:23:19AM -0700, Terion wrote:

> vagrant@server:~$ sudo add-apt-repository ppa:thumbor/ppa


So, you're trying to add `ppa:thumbor/ppa` but the one we provide is
`ppa:projectatomic/ppa`. Can you retry with the correct name please?
